blockingIssues.description: `types_compatible` still treats in-scope generic parameters as wildcards, and direct rerun probes confirmed unsound programs like `fn foo<T>(x: T) { takes_i32(x) }` and `fn bad<A, B>(a: A, b: B) -> A { b }` compile successfully.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether codegen features must always record an explicit manual `.rg -> .go` command in the handoff, or whether `compile_and_check`-style end-to-end coverage satisfies the manual-check requirement.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `phase-7-fix-non-addressable-pointer-receiver-regression` found the worker marked `followedProcedure` true while only recording test-suite validation, even though step 6 of the compiler-worker skill requires a representative manual `.rg -> .go` sanity-check command and output path.

rejectedObservations.observation: Re-raise the prior core-vs-later Phase 7 scope-overreach finding as a fresh blocking code defect in this rerun.
rejectedObservations.reason: guidance/accounting issue already captured in round 1 and explicitly superseded as non-feature follow-up guidance for this rerun.
